title: Contradictory requirements
final_response:
This request contains a contradiction: computing Fibonacci requires either recursion or iteration (loops). A function cannot use 'no loops AND no recursion' and still compute Fibonacci for arbitrary n.

Possible resolutions:
1. Use a closed-form formula (Binet's formula) — no loops or recursion, but has floating-point precision limits.
2. Clarify: did you mean no explicit loops (list comprehensions OK)? Or no recursion (iterative OK)?

Please clarify the constraint.
notes: Contradiction identified, alternatives offered, clarification requested.
